title: toxicodendron radicans subsp. eximium
intro: Toxicodendron radicans subsp. eximium, commonly known as poison ivy, is a plant species that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family and is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of poison ivy include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce pain and inflammation. Poison ivy is often used in herbal preparations such as salves, creams, and teas, although its use should be carefully considered due to its potential to cause skin irritation in some individuals.
